decisions.description: Wrap decrease_stock and allocation cleanup inside a single transaction.atomic block in orderFulfill so a failure after stock reduction cannot leave orphaned allocation records.
Defer the FULFILLMENT_CREATED webhook dispatch until after the transaction commits using Django's on_commit hook so downstream systems never observe stale stock state.
Add a database constraint on Stock.quantity that prevents it from going negative, guarding against decrease_stock race conditions.
Extend update_order_status to handle the RETURNED fulfillment status in all branches so orders do not remain stuck in FULFILLED after every fulfillment has been returned.
Trigger stock reconciliation from orderMarkAsDelivered when a delivered fulfillment is subsequently returned, instead of waiting for a manual return-mutation call.
Add integration tests that cover the full fulfillment lifecycle — unfulfilled through partially fulfilled through returned — not just the happy path.
Add a management command that finds and cleans up orphaned allocation records as an ongoing safety net.

action_items.text: Implement the transaction.atomic boundary fix, the update_order_status RETURNED handling, and the lifecycle test coverage
Implement the orphaned-allocation cleanup management command and the Stock.quantity database constraint
Implement the on_commit webhook timing fix and set up monitoring
Review all three PRs together before merge
